components.name: D-Glucose
components.evidence: grown in minimal media (M2) supplemented with 0.2 % glucose (M2G) according to Ely et al, which was modified by replacing MgCl2 with MgSO4.

components.name: Disodium hydrogen phosphate (Na2HPO4)
components.source: cited:Ely 1991 (M2 recipe as stated in Hottes 2004, PMC344409)
components.evidence: M2 minimal salts medium (6.1 mM Na2HPO4, 3.9 mM KH2PO4, 9.3 mM NH4Cl, 0.5 mM MgSO4, 10 uM FeSO4 [EDTA chelate; Sigma Chemical Co.], 0.5 mM CaCl2) with 0.2% glucose as the sole carbon source

components.name: Monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.source: cited:Ely 1991 (M2 recipe as stated in Hottes 2004, PMC344409)
components.evidence: M2 minimal salts medium (6.1 mM Na2HPO4, 3.9 mM KH2PO4, 9.3 mM NH4Cl, 0.5 mM MgSO4, 10 uM FeSO4 [EDTA chelate; Sigma Chemical Co.], 0.5 mM CaCl2) with 0.2% glucose as the sole carbon source

components.name: Phosphate (total, from Na2HPO4 + KH2PO4)
components.source: cited:Ely 1991 (M2 recipe as stated in Hottes 2004, PMC344409)
components.evidence: M2 minimal salts medium (6.1 mM Na2HPO4, 3.9 mM KH2PO4, ...) -- summed inorganic phosphate = 6.1 + 3.9 mM

components.name: Ammonium chloride (NH4Cl)
components.source: cited:Ely 1991 (M2 recipe as stated in Hottes 2004, PMC344409)
components.evidence: M2 minimal salts medium (6.1 mM Na2HPO4, 3.9 mM KH2PO4, 9.3 mM NH4Cl, 0.5 mM MgSO4, 10 uM FeSO4 [EDTA chelate; Sigma Chemical Co.], 0.5 mM CaCl2) with 0.2% glucose as the sole carbon source

components.name: Magnesium sulfate (MgSO4)
components.evidence: according to Ely et al, which was modified by replacing MgCl2 with MgSO4. (base M2: '...0.5 mM MgSO4...'). In this sulfur-metabolism study MgSO4 is the principal sulfate source.

components.name: Calcium chloride (CaCl2)
components.source: cited:Ely 1991 (M2 recipe as stated in Hottes 2004, PMC344409)
components.evidence: M2 minimal salts medium (6.1 mM Na2HPO4, 3.9 mM KH2PO4, 9.3 mM NH4Cl, 0.5 mM MgSO4, 10 uM FeSO4 [EDTA chelate; Sigma Chemical Co.], 0.5 mM CaCl2) with 0.2% glucose as the sole carbon source

components.name: Chloride (total, from NH4Cl + CaCl2)
components.source: cited:Ely 1991 (M2 recipe as stated in Hottes 2004, PMC344409)
components.evidence: 9.3 mM NH4Cl + 0.5 mM CaCl2 -> Cl- = 9.3 + (2 x 0.5) mM

components.name: Sulfate (total, from MgSO4 + FeSO4)
components.source: cited:Ely 1991 (M2 recipe as stated in Hottes 2004, PMC344409)
components.evidence: 0.5 mM MgSO4 + 10 uM FeSO4 -> SO4 = 0.5 + 0.01 mM (MgSO4 substituted for MgCl2 per paper's modification)

components.name: Iron(II) sulfate (FeSO4), EDTA-chelated
components.role: iron source (only trace metal in M2)
components.source: cited:Ely 1991 (M2 recipe as stated in Hottes 2004, PMC344409)
components.evidence: M2 minimal salts medium (... 10 uM FeSO4 [EDTA chelate; Sigma Chemical Co.] ...)

components.name: EDTA (ethylenediaminetetraacetate)
components.source: cited:Ely 1991 (M2 recipe as stated in Hottes 2004, PMC344409)
components.evidence: 10 uM FeSO4 [EDTA chelate; Sigma Chemical Co.] -- iron is supplied as a 1:1 FeSO4/EDTA chelate

components.name: Dioxygen (O2)
components.evidence: cultivated in Erlenmeyer shake flasks in Minitron incubators (Infors HT) with 150 rpm at 30 C -- Caulobacter crescentus is an obligate aerobe grown under vigorous aeration.

components.name: Water (H2O)
components.source: cited:Ely 1991 (M2 recipe as stated in Hottes 2004, PMC344409)

components.name: Proton (H+)
components.source: cited:Ely 1991 (M2 recipe as stated in Hottes 2004, PMC344409)
components.evidence: Na2HPO4/KH2PO4 phosphate buffer sets medium near neutral pH.

media.base_medium: M2 minimal salts medium (Ely 1991, Methods Enzymol 204:372-384), modified: MgCl2 replaced by MgSO4; + 0.2% glucose = M2G

sources_chased.what: base M2 recipe cited as ref 79 (Ely B, 1991, Genetics of Caulobacter crescentus, Methods Enzymol 204:372-384)
sources_chased.how: Ref 79 (Methods Enzymol, paywalled) recovered via primary Caulobacter methods paper that states the identical Ely M2 formulation verbatim: Hottes et al. 2004 J Bacteriol, WebFetch pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC344409/

missing_before: EDTA (iron chelator, present as the FeSO4/EDTA chelate)
media.notes: M2G is a fully defined minimal medium; the only trace metal is EDTA-chelated FeSO4 (10 uM). The current_components list contained spurious Fe3+ (EX_fe3_e), Co2+, Cu2+, Mn2+ and Zn2+ that are NOT part of authentic M2/M2G (no cobalt, copper, manganese, zinc or ferric salt is added) -- these should be dropped as reconstruction defaults. Oxygen corrected from 'facultative' to 'aerobic' (C. crescentus is an obligate aerobe cultivated in shake flasks at 150 rpm). All concentrations added; sulfate now derives from MgSO4 (the paper's deliberate MgCl2->MgSO4 swap), which is central to this sulfur/glutathione study. Recipe verbatim from Hottes et al. 2004 (PMC344409), which states the same Ely 1991 M2 formulation the paper cites.
